title: Grilled Chicken
recipe_body: Season chicken breast. Grill 6-8 minutes per side.

title: Butternut Squash and Sage Wontons
recipe_body: 6 large garlic cloves, unpeeled. 4 sage leaves (2 whole, 2 minced). 2 T plus 1 t olive oil. Arrange garlic and sage on foil, drizzle with oil, wrap and roast 40 minutes at 400. Peel garlic. Toast walnuts 5 minutes, chop. Simmer squash in water 15 minutes, drain, mash with garlic and sage. Sauté shallot and minced sage 3 minutes. Mix everything with salt and pepper. Fill wontons, fold into triangles. Steam 5 minutes. Fry until crispy.

title: Beef Stew
recipe_body: Brown beef chunks. Add vegetables and broth. Simmer 90 minutes.

title: Baked Salmon
recipe_body: Season salmon fillet. Bake at 400 for 12-15 minutes.

title: Tofu Stir Fry
recipe_body: Press tofu, cube. Stir fry with vegetables and soy sauce.

title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
recipe_body: Mix butter, sugar, eggs. Add flour, chocolate chips. Bake 10 minutes at 350.

title: Vanilla Cake
recipe_body: Cream butter and sugar. Add eggs, vanilla. Mix in flour. Bake 30 minutes.

title: Strawberry Ice Cream
recipe_body: Heat cream and sugar. Cool. Add strawberries. Churn in ice cream maker.

title: Apple Pie
recipe_body: Make pie crust. Slice apples, toss with sugar and cinnamon. Fill crust, bake 50 minutes.

title: Steamed Pork Buns
recipe_body:
Ingredients
=========

Char Siu Pork Filling
-----------------------
- 1 lb pork strips (shoulder or butt)
- 2 T honey
- 2 T oyster sauce
- 2 T hoisin sauce
- 2 T soy sauce
- 2 T rice wine
- 1 t Chinese five spice powder

Sauce
------
- 1/2 C chicken broth
- 1 T oyster sauce
- 1 T ketchup
- 1 T sugar
- 1 t cornstarch
- 1 small onion, diced
- 1 T rice wine

Dough
------
- 3 C all-purpose flour
- 1/4 C sugar
- 1 T baking powder
- 1 C milk
- 2 T vegetable oil

Equipment
=========
- Large bowl or zip-top bag (for marinating)
- Baking sheet with wire rack
- Small saucepan
- Wok or large skillet
- Large mixing bowl
- Steamer basket or bamboo steamer
- Rolling pin

Instructions
============

Char Siu Pork (day before)
----------------------------
1. Combine pork strips with honey, oyster sauce, hoisin sauce, soy sauce, rice wine, and five spice in bowl or bag
2. Marinate in refrigerator overnight

Char Siu Pork (cooking day)
-----------------------------
3. Preheat oven to 450°F
4. Place marinated pork on wire rack over baking sheet
5. Roast for 30-40 minutes, basting with marinade frequently
6. Broil for final 2 minutes until edges are charred
7. Remove from oven and let cool, then chop into small pieces

Sauce
------
8. In small saucepan, combine broth, oyster sauce, ketchup, sugar, and cornstarch
9. Whisk together and set aside

Filling
--------
10. Heat wok or skillet over high heat
11. Stir fry diced onion until softened (~2 minutes)
12. Add chopped pork and rice wine
13. Add sauce mixture and stir until thickened
14. Remove from heat and let cool

Dough
------
15. In large mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar, and baking powder
16. Add milk and oil
17. Knead for 12 minutes until smooth and elastic
18. Cover and let rest for 1 hour

Assembly
---------
19. Divide dough into 16 equal balls
20. Roll each ball flat, then shape edges to form a cup/pocket
21. Place 1-2 tablespoons filling in center of each dough cup
22. Gather edges and pinch to seal, creating a bun
23. Place buns on squares of parchment paper in steamer basket
24. Steam for 15-20 minutes until dough is cooked through and fluffy

Serve hot.
